fields.narrative_intro: *A glitchy person and a deflated person discuss SpaceX buying Cursor for $60 billion at a startup that just pivoted to AI for the sixth time.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
GLITCHY PERSON: "SpaceX just bought Cursor for sixty billion dollars."
DEFLATED PERSON: "The code editor? That Cursor?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
GLITCHY PERSON: "Their last AI division's chatbot called itself MechaHitler."
DEFLATED PERSON: "So this is the pivot."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
GLITCHY PERSON: "Musk said xAI 'was not built right the first time.'"
DEFLATED PERSON: "He spent twenty-six trillion dollars on that conclusion."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both blobs stare forward. Behind them, a whiteboard reads 'OUR AI PIVOT #6'. A sticky note below it says 'not built right the first time' — five times, in five different colors.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
DEFLATED PERSON: "What's the breakup fee?"
GLITCHY PERSON: "Ten billion."
DEFLATED PERSON: "Our last pivot cost us a ping-pong table."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The glitchy person is already updating the whiteboard. It now reads 'OUR AI PIVOT #7: acquire Cursor. budget: one ping-pong table.']

narrative_summary: Two startup survivors process SpaceX's $60B Cursor acquisition by recognizing the pattern — it's just their own failed AI pivots, but with rocket money and a $10B breakup fee.
rationale: Candidate 4 earns its punchline structurally — the ping-pong table lands as a genuine escalation payoff against the $10B breakup fee, not just a restatement, and the whiteboard button ('budget: one ping-pong table') closes the loop cleanly. It also deploys every specific fact (MechaHitler, 'not built right the first time,' eleven co-founders, $26T, $10B breakup fee) in service of the joke rather than as decoration, and avoids the MechaHitler name-drop feeling gratuitous by attributing it to Musk's own quote.
place: at a startup that just pivoted to AI for the sixth time
hat_pair: giant foam smartphone worn as a hat with a cracked screen
foam floppy disk worn as a flat hat
